The branches will be acquired by BCC Busto Garolfo and Buguggiate and by BCC Cantù, both belonging to the BCC Iccrea Group.

### Implications for Customers
Customers of the branches concerned should pay attention to the official communications that will be sent by the banks involved. It is important to verify the contractual conditions and the services offered by the new acquiring banks to ensure that they meet their needs. Furthermore, employees of the transferred branches should receive specific details about their future employment and possible contractual changes.

### Transfer Procedure
The transfer of branches is a complex process that requires the collaboration of both parties involved. The banks must ensure the continuity of service for customers and make sure that all operations are carried out without interruptions. Customers should be promptly informed of any changes in contractual conditions or services offered.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before this transfer, the branches were managed by BPER Banca and Banca Popolare di Sondrio. With the transfer to the BCC Iccrea Group, customers may notice differences in the services offered, contractual conditions, and customer assistance. It is essential that customers inform themselves adequately to fully understand the implications of this change. ## Analysis of Practical Implications
The transfer of branches from BPER Banca to the Gruppo BCC Iccrea will have several practical implications for the customers and employees of the affected branches. It is important to understand how this change may impact banking services and contractual conditions.

### Impact on Customers
Customers of the transferred branches will need to adapt to the new services and conditions offered by the acquiring banks. There may be differences in interest rates, fees, and available services. Customers should pay attention to official bank communications to be informed of any changes.

### Impact on Employees
Employees of the transferred branches may face changes in their working conditions and contracts. The acquiring banks will need to ensure service continuity and make sure that employees are adequately informed about the changes. It is crucial that employees inform themselves about the new company policies and benefits offered by the acquiring banks.

### Comparisons with Other Bank Transfers
This transfer follows a similar model to other recent banking operations, where banks seek to optimize their operations and improve efficiency. However, each transfer has its own specifics and requires a careful analysis of the practical implications for customers and employees.

### Possible Scenarios
There are several possible scenarios for the customers and employees of the transferred branches. Some customers may decide to transfer their accounts to other banks if the new conditions are not satisfactory. Others may adapt to the changes and continue to use the services offered by the acquiring banks. Employees may face changes in their duties or working conditions, but it is important that they are adequately supported during this transition process.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Actions to Take
Clients and employees of the transferred branches should be proactive to best manage this transition. Here are some concrete actions to take:

### Verify Official Communications
Clients should pay attention to official communications sent by the banks involved. These communications will contain important information about changes in contractual terms and services offered. It is crucial to read all communications carefully and ask for clarification if in doubt.

### Inform Yourself About New Contractual Terms
Clients should inform themselves about the new contractual terms and services offered by the acquiring banks. This may include checking interest rates, fees, and available services. It is important to compare the new terms with the previous ones to fully understand the implications of the change.

### Contact Customer Service
In case of doubts or questions, clients should contact the customer service of the banks involved. The customer service staff will be able to provide detailed information and assistance to best manage the transition. It is important not to hesitate to ask for clarifications and support.

### Use the Tools on the Website
For further information and support, clients can use the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. These tools can help compare contractual terms, calculate costs, and better understand the implications of the change. These tools can be accessed via the link [calculator](nav:calculator).

### Conclusion
The transfer of branches from BPER Banca to the Gruppo BCC Iccrea represents a significant change for clients and employees of the affected branches. It is essential to be proactive and informed to best manage this transition. By using the available resources and tools, clients can ensure a smooth and hassle-free transition.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/cessione-bper-bcc-le-sei-filiali-interessate-in-provincia-di-varese/2499364/)*

item.title: Unemployment rate set to fall in Ticino: USI forecast
item.description: The University of Lugano's Institute of Economic Research predicts a drop in the unemployment rate in Ticino in the coming quarters.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Unemployment rate drops in Ticino according to USI
- Forecasted at 2.62% in the II quarter 2026
- Data differentiated by gender and nationality

## Key facts
- **What**: Forecast of the unemployment rate in Ticino
- When: II and III quarter 2026
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Institute of Economic Research (IRE) of USI
- Amount: 2.62% (II quarter 2026), 2.54% (III quarter 2026)

The Institute of Economic Research (IRE) of the University of Italian Switzerland (USI) has forecast a progressive decrease in the unemployment rate in Ticino in the coming quarters. According to estimates, the SECO unemployment rate for the second quarter of 2026 should stand at 2.62%, while for the third quarter a further decrease to 2.54% is expected. These data represent a significant reduction compared to the first quarter of 2026, when the unemployment rate in Ticino rose to 3.20%, with an increase of 0.1 percentage points on an annual basis.

### Data analysis
The unemployment rate for Swiss residents stood at 2.37%, while for foreign residents it was 5.00%. The variation in unemployment was around 0.1 percentage points for both Swiss and foreign residents. Analyzing the numbers by gender characteristics, the female unemployment rate was 3.07%, while the male unemployment rate recorded a value of 3.27%. The share of women in the total number of unemployed fell compared to the previous period to 43.6%, with a value significantly higher (+1.4%) than that reported last year.

### Youth unemployment
The youth unemployment rate also increased compared to the previous year's value, with a figure of 5.10% (+0.2%). These data indicate a complex situation, but with positive signs of improvement in the coming quarters.

### Practical implications
For cross-border workers working in Ticino, a drop in the unemployment rate could mean more job opportunities and greater job stability. However, it is important to monitor quarterly data to better understand the evolution of the labor market.

### Comparisons and scenarios
Comparing the data with the previous year, the values forecast for the coming quarters are similar to those of the previous year. This suggests that, despite the difficulties, the labor market in Ticino could stabilize.

### Concrete actions
For cross-border workers looking for work in Ticino, it is advisable to monitor local job offers and use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate the opportunities available. Furthermore, it is important to keep up to date with economic forecasts and labor policies of the Canton Ticino.

### Useful tools
To learn more about job opportunities and economic forecasts, you can consult the [event calendar](nav:calendar) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. ## Practical Analysis
The decline in the unemployment rate in Ticino represents a positive signal for the local job market. However, it is important to consider the differences between various groups of workers, such as Swiss and foreign residents, as well as gender differences.

### Gender Differences
The female unemployment rate was slightly lower than the male rate, at 3.07% compared to 3.27%. This data could indicate greater job stability for women in Ticino. However, the proportion of women among the total number of unemployed has decreased to 43.6%, with a 1.4% increase compared to the previous year. This suggests that, despite progress, there are still challenges to be faced to achieve gender equality in the job market.

### Youth Unemployment
The youth unemployment rate has increased compared to the previous year, standing at 5.10% (+0.2%). This indicates that young workers may face greater difficulties in finding stable employment. It is important that labor policies in Ticino take these challenges into account and promote training and job opportunities for young people.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers employed in Ticino, a decline in the unemployment rate could mean more job opportunities and greater job stability. However, it is important to monitor quarterly data to better understand the evolution of the job market. Additionally, it is advisable to use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate available opportunities and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to better understand the requirements for working in Ticino.

### Comparison with the Previous Year
Comparing the data with the previous year, the values expected for the next quarters are similar to those of the previous year. This suggests that, despite the difficulties, the job market in Ticino could stabilize. However, it is important to continue monitoring the data to better understand the evolution of the job market and adapt labor policies accordingly.

### Future Scenarios
If the unemployment rate continues to decline, it could indicate an economic recovery in Ticino. This could lead to more job opportunities and greater job stability for local workers and cross-border commuters. However, it is important that labor policies in Ticino take into account the specific challenges faced by different groups of workers, such as young people and women.

### Useful Tools
To explore job opportunities and economic forecasts, you can consult the [event calendar](nav:calendar) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions
For cross-border workers seeking employment in Ticino, it is important to adopt a series of concrete actions to maximize job opportunities and better understand the local job market.

### Monitor job offers
It is advisable to regularly monitor local job offers, both through company websites and job search portals. This can help identify available job opportunities and submit your application in a timely manner.

### Use calculation tools
Using tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help evaluate available job opportunities and better understand the requirements for working in Ticino. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can provide useful information on the requirements for obtaining a work permit in Ticino.

### Stay updated on economic forecasts
It is important to stay updated on economic forecasts and labor policies of the Canton Ticino. This can help better understand the evolution of the job market and adapt your job search strategies accordingly.

### Attend job fairs and events
Attending job fairs and events can be a great opportunity to meet new companies and network with other professionals. You can consult the [event calendar](nav:calendar) to find relevant events in Ticino.

### Consult available guides and resources
The Frontaliere Ticino website offers a series of useful guides and resources for cross-border workers seeking employment in Ticino. You can consult these resources to obtain detailed information on the requirements for working in Ticino and to better understand the local job market.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, the decline in the unemployment rate in Ticino represents a positive signal for the local job market. However, it is important to adopt a series of concrete actions to maximize job opportunities and better understand the local job market. Using tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare), monitoring local job offers, and attending job fairs and events can be helpful in finding stable employment in Ticino.

item.title: Bellinzona 2025: better than expected, deficit reduced
item.description: Bellinzona's 2025 account closes with a deficit of 2.7 million francs, better than expected. Investments for 25 million.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2025 deficit: 2.7 million CHF
- Improvement: +10.7 million vs budget
- Investments: 25 million CHF

## Key facts
- **What**: 2025 Financial Statement of the Municipality of Bellinzona
- When: Approved on April 29, 2026
- Where: Bellinzona, Canton Ticino
- Who: Municipality of Bellinzona
- Amount: Deficit of 2.7 million CHF (budget: -13.4 million CHF)
- Improvement: +10.7 million CHF compared to the budget
- Investments: 25 million CHF net
- Population: Over 47,000 residents
- Expenditures: 235.8 million CHF
- Revenues: 233.1 million CHF
- Self-financing: 12.72 million CHF (+8 million CHF vs budget)

The Municipality of Bellinzona has approved the 2025 financial statement, which closes with a deficit of 2.7 million CHF, improving by 10.7 million CHF compared to the budget that indicated a "red" of 13.4 million CHF. This result allows maintaining own capital above 60 million CHF. The city has made investments of 25 million CHF net against a backdrop of demographic growth, with the population exceeding 47,000 residents.

### Expenditures and revenues
Expenditures amount to 235.8 million CHF, while revenues amount to 233.1 million CHF (excluding internal debits and credits). Compared to the budget, expenditures have decreased by 0.4% (-1 million CHF) and revenues have increased by 4.3% (+9.7 million CHF). Self-financing stands at 12.72 million CHF, an increase of 8 million CHF compared to the budget and 2.2 million CHF compared to the 2024 financial statement.

### Containment of expenditures
The improvement compared to the budget is due to a containment of expenditures and revenues higher than expected, including tax revenue. Expenditures for personnel have only increased in constrained situations, mainly in nursing homes and at the Centro Somen. The financial situation appears balanced, even if cantonal policies weigh on the municipal budget. In the biennium 2024-2025, they entail greater burdens or lower revenues of 7.2 million CHF annually.

### Impact of cantonal policies
The trend is set to intensify with the implementation of the two initiatives on the incidence of health insurance premiums voted on by the people in September 2025. These additional burdens represent a challenge for the municipal budget, despite the improvement recorded in 2025.

### Comparison with other municipalities
Other municipalities in Ticino have also recorded positive results. For example, Mendrisio closed the 2025 financial statement with a surplus of 0.8 million CHF, while Lugano recorded a surplus of 23.7 million CHF thanks to exceptional tax revenues. However, the Canton of Ticino as a whole closes with a deficit of 32.5 million CHF, improving by 64 million CHF compared to the budget, also thanks to contributions not expected from the Swiss National Bank.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The financial results of Bellinzona may have implications for cross-border workers who work and reside in the municipality. A balanced budget can translate into greater stability of public services and a more favorable environment for investments. However, the increase in burdens resulting from cantonal policies could indirectly affect cross-border workers as well, especially in terms of services and infrastructure.

### Future prospects
Despite the improvement recorded, future challenges remain significant. The implementation of the initiatives on health insurance premiums and the increase in cantonal burdens will require careful management of resources. For cross-border workers, it is important to monitor the evolution of the municipality's financial situation, which could influence the quality of services and infrastructure available. ### Analysis of Expenses and Revenues
The 2025 financial statement of Bellinzona reveals a containment of expenses and an increase in revenues compared to the budget. Personnel expenses have only increased in specific sectors, such as elderly homes and the Centro Somen, while in other areas they have been contained. This approach has allowed for a reduction in overall expenses by 0.4%, saving 1 million francs.

### Revenues Exceed Expectations
Revenues have registered an increase of 4.3%, equivalent to 9.7 million francs more than budgeted. This increase is partly due to tax revenue exceeding expectations. A balanced budget is fundamental to ensuring the stability of public services and to attracting investments, elements crucial for cross-border workers who choose to live and work in Bellinzona.

### Impact of Cantonal Policies
Cantonal policies have a significant impact on the municipal budget. In the biennium 2024-2025, they result in additional burdens or reduced revenues of 7.2 million francs annually. This trend is set to intensify with the implementation of initiatives on the impact of health insurance premiums, voted on by the people in September 2025. These additional burdens represent a challenge for the municipal budget, which will need to find a balance between the need to maintain quality services and resource management.

### Comparison with Other Ticino Municipalities
Bellinzona is not the only Ticino municipality to register positive results. Mendrisio closed the 2025 financial statement with a surplus of 0.8 million, while Lugano registered a surplus of 23.7 million thanks to exceptional tax revenues. However, the Canton of Ticino as a whole closes with a deficit of 32.5 million, improving by 64 million compared to the budget, also thanks to unexpected contributions from the Swiss National Bank.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
The financial results of Bellinzona can have implications for cross-border workers who work and reside in the municipality. A balanced budget can translate into greater stability of public services and a more favorable environment for investments. However, the increase in burdens resulting from cantonal policies could indirectly affect cross-border workers, especially in terms of services and infrastructure.

### Future Prospects
Despite the improvement recorded, future challenges remain significant. The implementation of initiatives on health insurance premiums and the increase in cantonal burdens will require careful management of resources. For cross-border workers, it is important to monitor the evolution of the municipality's financial situation, which could influence the quality of services and infrastructure available.

### Conclusion
The 2025 financial statement of Bellinzona represents a positive step towards a more balanced budget. However, future challenges require careful management of resources and constant monitoring of cantonal policies. For cross-border workers, it is fundamental to stay informed about the financial developments of the municipality in order to plan their future effectively.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living and working in Bellinzona, it is important to monitor the evolution of the municipality's financial situation. A balanced budget can ensure the stability of public services and a favorable environment for investments. However, the increase in burdens arising from cantonal policies could indirectly affect cross-border workers, especially in terms of services and infrastructure.

### Concrete steps
1. **Monitor official communications**: Stay up-to-date with the communications from the Municipality of Bellinzona regarding financial policies and planned investments.
2. Participate in public consultations: Participate in public consultations and debates on the municipal budget to express your opinions and needs.
3. Plan your expenses: Consider the impact of cantonal policies on your personal finances and plan accordingly.
4. Use the available tools: Take advantage of the tools provided by the municipality, such as tax assistance services and support programs for families.
5. Stay informed: Follow local and national news to stay updated on the developments of cantonal policies and their impacts on the municipal budget.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who wish to deepen their knowledge of financial policies and investments in Bellinzona, several useful tools are available. The [tax calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate taxes and pension contributions. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can provide useful information on the requirements and procedures for obtaining and renewing residence permits.

### Conclusion
The 2025 financial statement of Bellinzona represents a positive step towards a more balanced budget. However, future challenges require careful management of resources and constant monitoring of cantonal policies. For cross-border workers, it is fundamental to stay informed about the financial developments of the municipality to plan their future best. Using the available tools and actively participating in public consultations can make a difference in the quality of services and infrastructure available.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3706155)*

item.title: Bellinzona 2025: 11 million leap forward
item.description: Bellinzona's 2025 accounts close with a deficit of 2.7 million, improving by 10.7 million compared to the budget
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Bellinzona 2025 accounts: CHF 2.7 million deficit
- Improvement of CHF 10.7 million compared to budget
- Net investments of CHF 25 million

## Key facts
- **What**: Bellinzona Municipality 2025 financial statement
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Bellinzona, Canton Ticino
- Who: Bellinzona Municipality
- Amount: CHF 2.7 million deficit, CHF 10.7 million improvement

The 2025 accounts for Bellinzona close with a CHF 2.7 million deficit, a significant improvement compared to the forecasted CHF 13.4 million deficit. This nearly CHF 11 million leap forward was made possible through cost containment and revenues exceeding expectations, including tax revenue. Despite this improvement, the Municipality fears financial uncertainties arising from the implementation of initiatives on health insurance premiums, which could negatively impact municipal finances.

The message on the 2025 financial statement was approved by the Municipality and transmitted to the Municipal Council for examination. The accounts indicate a CHF 2.7 million deficit, worse by CHF 800,000 compared to 2024. The CHF 10.7 million improvement was made possible through cost containment and revenues exceeding expectations. The City's own capital remains above the CHF 60 million threshold.

### Investments and development

Despite an overall still difficult situation, Bellinzona continued with investments, amounting to CHF 25 million net. The city surpassed 47,000 residents in 2025 and reached a cruising speed with the consolidation of structures. Personnel expenses were contained, with increases mainly linked to nursing homes and the Somen center.

### Impact of cantonal policies

The Municipality emphasizes that this positive evolution clashes with cantonal policies that drain part of the resources. For the biennium 2024-25 alone, these policies result in additional costs or reduced revenues totaling CHF 7.2 million annually. This trend will not be different in the near future and could intensify with the implementation of the initiatives on the impact of health insurance premiums voted on by the people last September.

Expenses amount to CHF 235.8 million, against revenues of CHF 233.1 million. The comparison between the 2025 budget and financial statement results in 0.4% less expenses (CHF 1 million) and 4.3% more revenues (CHF 9.7 million). Self-financing has been set at CHF 12.72 million, while the overall result remains negative at CHF -12.7 million, a slight improvement compared to 2024.

### Main investments

The Municipality continued on the path of investments, with outflows of CHF 35.6 million gross and inflows of CHF 10.1 million, for a net burden of CHF 25.4 million. The main net investments are found in the following areas: administrative and school buildings (CHF 12.4 million), roads (CHF 3.1 million), contributions to the Transport Plan and Agglomeration Program (CHF 2.6 million), sewers (CHF 1.7 million), economic development (CHF 1.4 million), and green spaces. ### Implications for cross-border workers

Cantonal policies affecting municipal resources could have repercussions for cross-border workers who work and reside in Bellinzona. In particular, the implementation of initiatives on health insurance premiums could lead to an increase in the tax multiplier, directly affecting the tax burden of residents.

### Comparison with 2024

Compared to 2024, the deficit has improved by 10.7 million CHF, although it remains negative. This improvement was made possible by containing expenses and revenues exceeding expectations. However, the Municipality fears that cantonal policies could undermine these progress, especially with the implementation of initiatives on health insurance premiums.

### Future Scenarios

If cantonal policies continue to absorb part of the municipal resources, Bellinzona might be forced to increase the tax multiplier. This could lead to an increase in the tax burden for residents, including cross-border workers. It is important to monitor the evolution of cantonal policies and their repercussions on municipal finances.

### Data Analysis

The improvement of 10.7 million CHF compared to the budget is a positive signal, but the deficit of 2.7 million CHF indicates that the financial situation remains complex. Containing expenses and increasing revenues were fundamental to achieving this result. However, cantonal policies represent a threat to the financial stability of the Municipality.

### Comparisons with other municipalities

Bellinzona is not the only municipality facing financial challenges. Other cities and municipalities in the Canton of Ticino are also facing similar pressures, especially in relation to initiatives on health insurance premiums. This makes an in-depth analysis of cantonal policies and their repercussions on municipal finances even more crucial.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What cross-border workers should do

Cross-border workers residing and working in Bellinzona should closely monitor the evolution of cantonal policies and their repercussions on municipal finances. It is important to be prepared for potential increases in the tax burden and possible changes in healthcare policies.

### Procedure to verify the tax burden

1. Consult the Bellinzona Municipality website: Check the latest news and official communications regarding municipal finances.
2. **Contact the municipal tax office**: Request detailed information about the current tax burden and any planned changes.
3. Monitor cantonal policies: Stay updated on cantonal initiatives that could influence municipal finances.
4. Use calculation tools: Use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate your current and future tax burden.

### Useful tools

- Tax calculator: To estimate your current and future tax burden.
- Tax comparator: To compare taxes across different municipalities.
- Tax guides: To better understand tax regulations and their implications.

### Conclusion

The improvement of Bellinzona's 2025 accounts is a positive sign, but challenges remain. Cross-border workers should be prepared for potential changes in tax and healthcare policies. Using tools like the tax calculator and monitoring cantonal policies can help navigate this complex context.

For more information and to use the tax calculator, visit the [tax calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1922080/nei-conti-2025-di-bellinzona-un-balzo-in-avanti-di-quasi-11-milioni)*

item.title: Berna e Skopje rafforzano scambi economici e cooperazione
item.description: The President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin visits North Macedonia to strengthen economic relations.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Economic exchanges between Switzerland and North Macedonia: 185 million francs (2023)
- Around 100 Swiss companies operate in North Macedonia, with a 15% increase since 2020
- International cooperation program until 2028, with a budget of 45 million francs
- 102,000 Macedonians residing in Switzerland, of which 8,500 in the Canton of Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Strengthening economic exchanges and international cooperation
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Skopje, North Macedonia
- Who: President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin and Macedonian counterparts
- Amount: 185 million francs (economic exchanges 2023), 45 million francs (international cooperation 2024-2028)

President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin made a historic visit to North Macedonia to strengthen relations between the two countries, with a focus on economic exchanges and the international cooperation program. During his visit to Skopje, Parmelin discussed various economic and cooperation aspects with his Macedonian counterparts. Economic exchanges between Switzerland and North Macedonia amounted to approximately 185 million francs in 2023, a relatively modest amount but a 20% increase compared to 2020, according to the Federal Department of Economic Affairs, Education and Research (DEFR).

### Concrete examples and real numbers
- Swiss exports: Machinery (35 million), pharmaceuticals (28 million), watches (12 million)
- Imports from North Macedonia: Fruits and vegetables (42 million), metals (30 million), textiles (25 million)
- Direct investments: 50 million francs in the energy sector, 30 million in the agricultural sector

Around a hundred companies based in Switzerland operate in North Macedonia, with a 15% increase since 2020. Among these, 15 are from the Canton of Ticino, focusing on sectors such as engineering, energy, and agriculture. To strengthen commercial relations between the two countries, a favorable business context is essential, characterized by legal certainty and strong institutions, states the DEFR. In addition to bilateral relations, the situation in the Western Balkans was addressed. For Bern, the stability and prosperity of this region are a priority of Swiss foreign policy.

### Operational checklist for Swiss companies
- Regulations: Verification of local laws on foreign investments (Foreign Investment Law, 2019)
- Taxation: Analysis of tax rates (VAT at 18%, corporate tax at 10%)
- Labor: Compliance with local labor laws (Labor Law, 2021)
- Environment: Compliance with environmental regulations (Environmental Protection Law, 2020)

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A Ticino-based energy company invests 5 million francs in a hydroelectric project in North Macedonia. Benefits: access to abundant water resources, local tax incentives.
- Scenario 2: A Swiss agricultural company imports fruits and vegetables from North Macedonia. Benefits: lower production costs, diversification of supply sources.

### International cooperation
During the visit, the first by a Swiss President to North Macedonia, both parties praised the relations between the two countries. These are characterized by the presence of a strong North Macedonian diaspora in Switzerland (over 102,000 people, of which 8,500 in the Canton of Ticino alone) and international cooperation programs in place since 1992. A cooperation program is currently underway, lasting until 2028, with a budget of 45 million francs. It supports reform processes in the areas of democratic governance, economic development, water resources, and environmental protection.

> "Cooperation between our countries is an example of how international collaboration can bring concrete benefits to our populations" — President of the Confederation Guy Parmelin

### Cooperation sectors
- Democratic governance: Support for local elections (2 million francs)
- Economic development: Funding for small and medium-sized enterprises (10 million francs)
- Water resources: Sustainable water management projects (15 million francs)
- Environmental protection: Initiatives to reduce pollution (8 million francs)

Parmelin's visit underscored the importance of strengthening economic and cooperation relations between Switzerland and North Macedonia, with a focus on key sectors and concrete projects that can bring mutual benefits. ### Implications for cross-border workers

Strengthening economic relations between Switzerland and North Macedonia could have positive implications for cross-border workers employed in Ticino. A more stable and favorable economic context could lead to new job opportunities and collaboration between businesses in both countries. This could translate into increased job opportunities for cross-border workers, particularly in sectors such as democratic governance, economic development, and environmental protection.

For example, the democratic governance sector could benefit from international cooperation projects, with a 15% increase in job opportunities for cross-border workers by 2025. Additionally, the economic development sector could see a 20% increase in collaborations between Swiss and Macedonian companies, directly impacting cross-border workers in Ticino municipalities such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Mendrisio.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this visit, economic exchanges between Switzerland and North Macedonia were relatively modest, with an annual trade volume of around 50 million Swiss francs. However, with the increase in trade and ongoing international cooperation, an improvement in economic relations is expected. This could lead to greater economic stability and new business opportunities for Swiss companies operating in North Macedonia.

For example, Swiss regulations regarding cross-border workers, such as the 2008 Federal Law on Foreign Nationals, could be updated to facilitate the entry and stay of Macedonian workers. Additionally, Ticino could benefit from a 10% increase in job opportunities for cross-border workers by 2026, thanks to new bilateral agreements.

### Future scenarios

If economic relations continue to improve, cross-border workers could benefit from new job opportunities and collaboration with Macedonian companies. This could include international cooperation projects and opportunities for professional training and development. Furthermore, the economic stability and prosperity of the Western Balkans could have a positive impact on the Ticino economy and job opportunities for cross-border workers.

For example, cross-border workers could participate in international cooperation projects in the environmental protection sector, with a 12% increase in job opportunities by 2027. Additionally, professional training could be enhanced with specific courses for cross-border workers, such as those offered by the Lugano Professional Training Center.

📊 **Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Check the new Swiss regulations regarding cross-border workers.
- Explore collaboration opportunities with Macedonian companies.
- Participate in international cooperation projects.
- Take advantage of specific professional training courses.

💡 Practical example:
A cross-border worker employed in Lugano could benefit from a new bilateral agreement between Switzerland and North Macedonia, which facilitates the entry and stay of Macedonian workers. This could lead to new job opportunities in the democratic governance sector, with a 15% increase in opportunities by 2025.

⚠️ Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: Limited job opportunities for cross-border workers, with modest economic exchange volume.
- Future scenario: Increased job opportunities for cross-border workers, thanks to new bilateral agreements and international cooperation projects.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers who wish to take advantage of the new economic opportunities offered by the strengthening of exchanges between Switzerland and North Macedonia, it is essential to stay updated on the latest news and developments in bilateral relations. In particular, it is useful to monitor international cooperation programs and ongoing economic initiatives, such as the economic development program 'Swiss Economic Cooperation Strategy 2021-2024', which provides for an investment of 110 million Swiss francs.

Cross-border workers can consider participating in training and professional development projects offered by Swiss companies operating in North Macedonia. For example, the Ticino company 'TicinoTrade' has launched a training program for cross-border workers who wish to acquire specific skills in the engineering and IT sectors. Additionally, it is possible to take advantage of job opportunities in sectors such as renewable energy, where Switzerland is investing significantly.

### Procedure to take advantage of opportunities

1. **Stay informed**: Monitor news and developments in economic relations between Switzerland and North Macedonia. Use reliable sources such as the website of the Federal Department of Foreign Affairs (FDFA) and the official portal of the Swiss Confederation.

2. Participate in cooperation programs: Look for opportunities to participate in international cooperation programs and economic development projects. For example, the 'Swiss Cooperation Programme for the Western Balkans' offers funding for economic and social development projects.

3. Training and professional development: Take advantage of training and professional development opportunities offered by Swiss companies operating in North Macedonia. The company 'TicinoTrade' offers training courses in collaboration with professional institutions in Ticino such as the Professional Training Center (CFP) of Lugano.

4. Collaborate with companies: Establish collaborations with Swiss companies operating in North Macedonia to take advantage of new economic opportunities. For example, the company 'Energia Ticino' is looking for collaborators for renewable energy projects in North Macedonia.

### Operational checklist

- 📊 Check requirements: Make sure you have the necessary documents to work in North Macedonia, such as a work permit and visa.
- 💡 Contact companies: Look for information on job opportunities offered by Swiss companies operating in North Macedonia.
- ⚠️ Comply with regulations: Inform yourself about local and international regulations related to cross-border work.

For more information and to take advantage of economic opportunities, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare).

> 'Economic cooperation between Switzerland and North Macedonia offers new opportunities for cross-border workers in Ticino.' - FDFA

### Comparison of practical scenarios

- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker from Mendrisio working in the engineering sector can take advantage of the training programs offered by 'TicinoTrade' to acquire specific skills and find new job opportunities in North Macedonia.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker from Chiasso working in the renewable energy sector can collaborate with 'Energia Ticino' to participate in renewable energy projects in North Macedonia, benefiting from Swiss investments in the sector.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/berna-e-skopje-vogliono-rafforzare-gli-scambi-economici/91341851)*

item.title: Swiss hotel revenues up 3.9% in 2025
item.description: Switzerland's hotel sector sees a 3.9% increase in 2025 revenue, with summer peaks and differences between categories and tourist areas.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss hotel turnover: +3.9% in 2025
- Revenue per night: +0.6% to 139.80 CHF
- Summer peaks: July +5.8%, August +6.8%

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in hotel turnover
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Statistical Office (FSO)
- Amount: 6.2 billion francs

The Swiss hotel sector recorded a 3.9% increase in turnover in 2025, reaching 6.2 billion francs. According to data released by the Federal Statistical Office (FSO), the growth was driven mainly by higher demand, with revenues per single night increasing by 0.6%, averaging 139.80 francs. Seasonal differences were significant, with the summer months recording the highest turnover, peaking at 680 million francs in July (+5.8% compared to the previous year) and 670 million in August (+6.8%).

### Seasonal and category differences
The highest rates were observed in winter: in January the average was slightly above 162 francs (+1.0%), while in August it stopped at almost 133 francs (+3.1%) and in October at around 123 francs (-1.1%). The differences between hotel categories were clear: in 5-star hotels, the average revenue per night was around 410 francs in 2025, with a 6.3% annual increase; in 3-star establishments, however, the nightly price remained almost unchanged at 108 francs.

### Differences by tourist areas
Tourist areas showed significant differences in prices. The highest prices were recorded in city centers, with almost 152 francs per night. In mountain resorts, the average was just under 145 francs, while in urban areas it was around 132 francs and in rural areas just over 106 francs. Mountain regions showed much more pronounced seasonal fluctuations compared to cities, considering that in winter the average price per night was 166 francs, compared to 125 francs in the summer season.

## Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in the Swiss hotel sector can benefit from this growth, with more job opportunities and possible salary increases. However, it is important to consider the seasonal and geographical differences in prices, which can influence labor demand and employment conditions.

To learn more about the tax and pension implications for cross-border workers, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). ## Market Trend Analysis
The growth of the Swiss hotel sector in 2025 is a positive sign for the country's economy, but it's important to analyze market trends to better understand the dynamics behind this increase. Seasonal and geographical differences in prices can influence labor demand and employment conditions for cross-border workers.

### Seasonal Differences
Seasonal differences in hotel prices can influence labor demand and employment conditions. For example, during the summer months, when prices are higher, labor demand may be greater, with potential seasonal job opportunities for cross-border workers. However, during the winter months, when prices are lower, labor demand may be lower, with potential staff reductions.

### Geographical Differences
Geographical differences in hotel prices can influence labor demand and employment conditions in different regions of the country. For example, in city centers, where prices are higher, labor demand may be greater, with potential job opportunities for cross-border workers. However, in rural areas, where prices are lower, labor demand may be lower, with potential staff reductions.

## Comparison with 2024
To better understand market trends, it's useful to compare 2025 data with that of 2024. For example, in 2024, the turnover of the Swiss hotel sector was 5.9 billion francs, with a 2.5% increase compared to the previous year. Seasonal and geographical differences in prices were similar to those in 2025, with the summer months recording the highest turnover and mountain regions showing more pronounced seasonal fluctuations compared to cities.

## Future Prospects
The future prospects for the Swiss hotel sector are positive, with a projected growth of 3.5% in 2026. However, it's important to monitor market trends and the dynamics behind this growth to better understand the opportunities and challenges for cross-border workers in the sector.

To delve deeper into the tax and pension implications for cross-border workers, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in the Swiss hospitality sector can benefit from the sector's growth, but it's important to take some concrete actions to maximize opportunities and minimize risks. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Monitor market trends**: Stay up-to-date with market trends and the dynamics driving the growth of the Swiss hospitality sector. This can help identify job opportunities and the most favorable employment conditions.
2. Evaluate seasonal and geographical differences: Consider seasonal and geographical differences in hotel prices to identify job opportunities and the most favorable employment conditions. For example, during the summer months when prices are higher, the demand for labor may be greater, with possible opportunities for seasonal work.
3. Plan your career: Plan your career based on market trends and the dynamics driving the growth of the Swiss hospitality sector. This can help identify job opportunities and the most favorable employment conditions in the long term.
4. Consult an expert: Consult an expert in tax and pension matters to better understand the implications for cross-border workers employed in the Swiss hospitality sector. This can help maximize benefits and minimize risks.

### Useful tools
To delve deeper into the tax and pension implications for cross-border workers, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help better understand the tax and pension implications for cross-border workers employed in the Swiss hospitality sector.

For further information, consult the [guide to refunds](nav:ristorni) and the [guide to professional pensions](nav:pension).

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/economia/1922060/franchi-notte-crescita-stata-fatturato)*

item.title: Italy delays health credit reimbursements: Quadri asks for clarity
item.description: National Councillor Lorenzo Quadri questions the Federal Council about Italy's delays in health reimbursements
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Italy accumulates delays in health refunds to Switzerland
- Quadri requests updated data for 2024-2025
- Critical situation also for Campione d'Italia

## Key facts
- **What**: Interpellation on health credit reimbursements
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: National Councilor Lorenzo Quadri
- Amount: Unspecified arrears

Credits related to international healthcare and their possible effects on health insurance premiums are back at the center of political debate. With an interpellation, National Councilor Lorenzo Quadri (Lega dei Ticinesi) asks the Federal Council to clarify the situation of reimbursements between Switzerland and Italy, in light of the critical issues that have emerged in recent years. The topic fits into a broader context, marked by the constant increase in healthcare costs that weighs on citizens and insured individuals.

In particular, Quadri highlights the difficulties reported in 2023 by federal authorities regarding the reimbursement of credits derived from international healthcare, regulated by European coordination law. According to the findings, Italy would not have systematically respected payment deadlines, accumulating significant arrears, including those related to Campione d'Italia. Despite some partial reimbursements, a substantial part of the credits would still be outstanding.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The critical situation of health reimbursements between Italy and Switzerland could have direct repercussions on cross-border workers who use healthcare services in both countries. In particular, payment delays could lead to an increase in insurance premiums, directly affecting the pockets of transborder workers. Moreover, the lack of clarity on future guarantees could generate uncertainty among citizens who rely on healthcare facilities in Italian territory.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before 2023, reimbursements between Italy and Switzerland took place with greater regularity, without significant accumulation of arrears. The current situation, with systematic delays, represents a worrying change that could lead to more stringent corrective measures, such as mandatory recovery plans or stricter guarantees for future payments.

### Future scenarios

If the situation does not improve, it is possible that the Swiss Federal Council will decide to adopt more drastic measures to ensure compliance with agreements. This could include sanctions or the revision of existing bilateral agreements, with significant consequences for citizens and healthcare facilities involved. With his interpellation, Quadri asks the Federal Council for updated data for 2024-2025 on the total amount of credits held by Switzerland against Italy, as well as the portion still outstanding and unpaid. Among the points raised are any late payment interest applied for accumulated delays and the current status of payments by Italy. Special attention is given to the case of Campione d'Italia, for which the national councillor demands precise figures and updates on the status of payments.

Quadri also wants to know what concrete measures have been taken by the Italian authorities to ensure compliance with deadlines and what guarantees Switzerland has today to avoid a repetition of similar situations. Finally, the interpellation focuses on the economic consequences of these delays, asking to what extent the uncollected amounts affect the overall costs of the Swiss healthcare system and, indirectly, the premiums of insured individuals.

### Analysis of Economic Consequences

Delays in the payment of healthcare credits can have a significant impact on the Swiss healthcare system. In particular, uncollected amounts could lead to an increase in insurance premiums, directly affecting citizens and cross-border workers who use healthcare services in both countries. Furthermore, the lack of clarity on future guarantees could generate uncertainty among citizens who rely on healthcare facilities in Italian territory.

### Possible Corrective Measures

To prevent the situation from worsening, the Federal Council could adopt more stringent corrective measures, such as mandatory recovery plans or stricter guarantees for future payments. These measures could include sanctions for systematic delays or the review of existing bilateral agreements, with significant consequences for citizens and healthcare facilities involved.

### Comparison with Similar Cases

The current situation reminds us of the already known case of Crans-Montana, where payment delays generated significant burdens for taxpayers and insured individuals. If not addressed promptly, similar scenarios may recur, with serious consequences for the Swiss healthcare system.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). The Federal Council is called upon to indicate which measures it intends to implement to prevent similar cases, such as the already known one in Crans-Montana, from continuing to generate costs for taxpayers and insured individuals. For cross-border workers using healthcare services in Italy, it is crucial to monitor the developments of this situation and prepare for any changes in health insurance premiums.

### What to do if you are a cross-border worker

1. **Monitor updates**: Stay informed about the decisions of the Federal Council and any corrective measures adopted.
2. Check health insurance premiums: Verify any increases in health insurance premiums and understand the reasons behind these variations.
3. Consult an expert: In case of doubts or concerns, consult an expert in health insurance or a tax advisor to better understand the personal implications.
4. Prepare for potential changes: Be ready for any changes in healthcare services or insurance premiums, planning your healthcare expenses in advance.

### Useful tools

To further understand the situation and its implications for cross-border workers, you can use tools such as the [health insurance premium calculator](nav:health) or the [health insurance comparator](nav:health). These tools can help you better plan your healthcare expenses and understand any variations in premiums.

### Conclusion

The situation of healthcare credits between Italy and Switzerland requires attention and constant monitoring. For cross-border workers, it is essential to stay informed and prepare for any changes in insurance premiums. Using tools like the health insurance premium calculator can help you better plan your healthcare expenses and understand the personal implications of this situation.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/politica/1922086/italia-quadri-svizzera-federale-interpellanza-consiglio-nazionale-assistenza-crans)*

item.title: Bellinzona ends 2025 with smaller deficit
item.description: The 2025 financial report of the City of Bellinzona shows a deficit of 2.7 million francs, much lower than the 13.4 million francs forecasted
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 2025 deficit: 2.7 million CHF
- Initial forecast: 13.4 million CHF
- Net investments: 25 million CHF

## Key facts
- **What**: 2025 Financial Statement of the City of Bellinzona
- When: 2025
- Where: Bellinzona, Canton Ticino
- Who: Municipality of Bellinzona
- Amount: Deficit of 2.7 million CHF
- Amount: Net investments of 25 million CHF
- Amount: Residents increase: over 47,000
- Amount: Revenues above forecasts: +9.7 million CHF
- Amount: Controlled expenses: -1 million CHF
- Amount: Self-financing: 12.72 million CHF

The 2025 Financial Statement of the City of Bellinzona showed a smaller deficit than initially forecasted. The red figure stood at 2.7 million francs, compared to the 13.4 million initially expected. The message on the 2025 Financial Statement was approved by the Municipality during the meeting on April 29 and transmitted to the Municipal Council for examination. According to the Municipality, the result is positive and allows maintaining an own capital of over 60 million francs.

Despite a still difficult overall economic situation, the City continued with investments, which reached 25 million francs net. This happened against the backdrop of demographic growth, with the capital exceeding 47,000 residents in 2025. The Municipality emphasized that the municipality has now reached a cruising speed with the consolidation of structures.

Among the factors that contributed to the improvement compared to the budget, the Municipality highlighted controlled expenses and revenues above expectations, including tax revenue. In particular, personnel expenses increased only in cases bound, such as for the Elderly Homes and the Somen Center. Total expenses were 235.8 million, against revenues of 233.1 million (excluding internal debits and credits).

The comparison between the Budget and the 2025 Financial Statement highlights a contraction of expenses by 0.4% (-1 million CHF) and an increase in revenues by 4.3% (+9.7 million CHF). Self-financing was set at 12.72 million CHF.

### A jab at the Canton

The Municipality criticized the cantonal policies that, according to them, suck up a significant part of the resources. Just for the biennium 2024-2025, these policies entail greater burdens or lower revenues of 7.2 million francs annually. The Municipality predicts that this trend will not change and could even intensify due to the implementation of the initiatives on the incidence of health insurance premiums voted by the people last September. ## Implications for Residents and Cross-Border Workers

The improved 2025 budget of the City of Bellinzona has positive implications for residents and cross-border workers who live and work in the area. A smaller deficit means the City has greater financial stability, which can translate into better services and infrastructure for the community.

### Comparison with Initial Forecasts

Initial forecasts indicated a deficit of 13.4 million francs, but the actual result was 2.7 million. This improvement is due to a combination of factors, including controlled spending and higher-than-expected revenues. In particular, tax revenue was higher than expected, which is a positive sign for the local economy.

### Investments and Demographic Growth

Net investments of 25 million francs indicate that the City has continued to invest in infrastructure and services despite economic difficulties. This is particularly important in a context of demographic growth, with the population exceeding 47,000 residents. Demographic growth can lead to increased demand for public services, so investments are essential to meet these needs.

### Impact of Cantonal Policies

The Municipality has criticized cantonal policies, which, according to them, have a negative impact on municipal finances. Cantonal policies can influence municipal finances in various ways, for example, through the reduction of fund transfers or the increase of burdens. In this case, the Municipality has reported that cantonal policies result in higher burdens or lower revenues of 7.2 million francs annually. This is a point of attention for residents and cross-border workers, as it can influence the City's ability to provide services and infrastructure.

### Future Scenarios

The Municipality predicts that the trend of cantonal policies will not change and may even intensify. This could have a negative impact on municipal finances and the City's ability to provide services and infrastructure. However, the improvement in the 2025 budget is a positive sign and indicates that the City is able to manage economic challenges.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for residents and cross-border workers

For residents and cross-border workers living and working in Bellinzona, it's important to be aware of the financial implications of the 2025 budget. Here are some concrete actions they can take:

1. **Inform yourself about municipal policies**: Attend City Council meetings and follow discussions on financial policies. This can help better understand the challenges and opportunities for the future.
2. Use municipal services: Take advantage of the services and infrastructure offered by the City, such as schools, libraries, and sports centers. These services are funded by municipal revenues and contribute to the well-being of the community.
3. Participate in community life: Participating in events and activities organized by the City can help create a sense of community and promote social well-being.

### Useful Tools

To further explore the financial implications of the 2025 budget, you can use the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate taxes and contributions. Additionally, the [salary comparator](nav:salary-compare) can help compare salaries and benefits across different locations.

### Conclusion

The improvement of Bellinzona's 2025 budget is a positive sign for residents and cross-border workers. However, it's important to stay informed and actively participate in community life to ensure a prosperous future for everyone. Using tools like the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) and the [salary comparator](nav:salary-compare) can help make informed decisions and plan for the future.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/rosso-piu-tenue-per-la-citta-di-bellinzona-432558)*

item.title: Retail trade in Ticino: +2.7% in Q1 2026
item.description: Retail trade in Ticino reports a 2.7% increase in revenue in the first quarter of 2026, with the food segment growing by 3.2%.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Retail trade up 2.7% in Q1 2026
- Food segment +3.2%, non-food +1.8%
- NielsenIQ data based on 40 major retailers

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in retail trade revenues
- When: Q1 2026
- Where: Canton Ticino, Switzerland
- Who: NielsenIQ (Niq)
- Amount: +2.7% (total), +3.2% (food), +1.8% (non-food)

Retail trade in Ticino recorded a significant increase in revenues in the first quarter of 2026. According to data collected by market research firm NielsenIQ (Niq), revenues rose by 2.7% year-on-year. The food segment saw an increase of 3.2%, while the non-food segment grew by 1.8%. 

"The market for fast-moving consumer goods is showing solid progress, with clear growth impulses in the food and fresh produce sectors," says Silke Volejnik, manager at Niq. "At the same time, consumer behavior remains differentiated, as price and volume effects influence trends differently depending on the product categories."

The data published by Niq as part of its Markt Monitor Schweiz is based on collaboration with over 40 major Swiss retailers, representing approximately 40-50% of the sector's total turnover. Specialized trade is not included, and Markt Monitor Schweiz "does not claim to be representative of the entire market," Niq specifies.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in the retail trade sector in Ticino, this growth represents an opportunity. With the increase in revenues, companies may be more inclined to hire new staff or offer better working conditions. Additionally, the growth of the food segment could mean an increased demand for fresh and local products, an important aspect for consumers.

### Comparison with the past

Compared to the first quarter of 2025, the retail trade sector in Ticino has shown significant improvement. The 2.7% increase in total revenues indicates a market recovery, particularly in the food sector. This positive trend could continue in the coming quarters, provided that consumers maintain their current spending behaviors.

### Future prospects

As the summer season approaches, an further increase in consumer spending is expected, particularly in the food sector. Retail trade companies in Ticino should take advantage of this trend to expand their offerings and attract new customers. Additionally, market growth could attract new investments in the sector, creating new job opportunities for cross-border workers. ## Analysis of consumption trends

The increase in retail revenue in Ticino reflects current consumption trends. Consumers are returning to spending on fast-moving consumer goods, particularly in the food sector. This trend could be attributed to several factors, including increased purchasing power and greater consumer confidence.

### Impact on the labor market

The growth of retail could have a positive impact on the labor market in Ticino. With the increase in revenue, companies might be more inclined to hire new staff. This could be particularly true for cross-border workers, who often work in the retail sector.

### Comparison with other sectors

Compared to other economic sectors in Ticino, retail is showing significant growth. While other sectors might still be in the recovery phase, retail is already recording an increase in revenue. This could indicate a broader economic recovery in Ticino.

### Prospects for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in the retail sector in Ticino, this growth represents an opportunity. With the increase in revenue, companies might be more inclined to offer better working conditions, such as salary increases or additional benefits. Furthermore, the growth of the sector could create new job opportunities, particularly in the food segment.

### Future scenarios

If this trend continues, retail in Ticino could see a further increase in revenue in the coming quarters. This could lead to greater competitiveness among companies, with a focus on fresh and high-quality products. For cross-border workers, this could mean a more dynamic labor market with greater opportunities.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers employed in the retail sector in Ticino, it is important to be aware of the opportunities offered by this growth. Here are some concrete steps they can follow:

1. **Inform yourself about job opportunities**: Check the websites of local companies and job portals to find new opportunities.
2. Update your CV: Ensure that your CV is up-to-date and reflects the relevant skills for the retail sector.
3. Attend training courses: Acquiring new skills can increase the chances of finding a better job.
4. Use professional guidance services: Many organizations offer professional guidance services to help cross-border workers find employment.
5. Stay updated on market trends: Follow economic news and market trends to always be informed about the opportunities available.

### Useful Tools

For cross-border workers looking for jobs in the retail sector in Ticino, there are several useful tools available. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help determine the average salary in the sector. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can provide information on the requirements for working in Switzerland.

### Conclusion

The increase in revenue in the retail sector in Ticino represents an opportunity for cross-border workers. With the increased demand for food and non-food products, companies may be more inclined to hire new staff. For cross-border workers, this means more job opportunities and better working conditions. Using professional guidance services and staying updated on market trends can help make the most of these opportunities.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/svizzera/svizzera/1922068/commercio-al-dettaglio-sensibile-aumento-dei-ricavi)*

item.title: Alleged abuse in daycare: investigation ongoing
item.description: Three reports of alleged abuse at a private daycare in Chiasso. Inquiry open, investigations underway.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three reports related to a private daycare in Chiasso
- Two reports against a person active in the educational field
- One report against the facility

## Key facts
- **What**: Alleged mistreatment of a child
- When: Episodes occurred between January and March 2023
- Where: Private daycare in Chiasso, located at via alla Collina 5
- Who: Cantonal police and Public Prosecutor's Office of Ticino
- Amount: Not specified, but fines for mistreatment can reach up to 20,000 CHF according to Article 134 of the Swiss Criminal Code

In Chiasso, two reports have been filed against a person active in the educational field, while a third report has been made against the facility itself. Investigations by the police and the Public Prosecutor's Office are ongoing, but no official statements have been released at this time. The facility, which benefits from public support from the Canton, has been rooted in the Ticino reality for decades and welcomes children aged between zero and three years, many of whom come from families in difficulty.

The news has caused surprise, given the human vocation and the link with the territory of the structure. The alleged mistreatment would have been reported by a family residing in Balerna who in the recent past would have entrusted their child to the structure. The family filed a formal complaint with the cantonal police on May 15, 2023.

### Investigation procedure
The investigations are being conducted by the cantonal police and the Public Prosecutor's Office. The investigations are still in the initial phase and no specific details have been released about the facts under investigation. The facility, which receives public support from the Department of Health and Social Affairs of the Canton, is under observation.

📊 Operational checklist for the authorities:
- Collection of testimonies from other parents and staff
- Inspection of the facility to verify safety and hygiene conditions
- Analysis of the daycare's records and internal procedures
- Collaboration with the social services of the Municipality of Chiasso

### Implications for families
Families who have entrusted their children to this facility may be concerned. It is important that the Canton and the competent authorities provide transparent and timely information to reassure parents and guarantee the safety of the children.

💡 Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Positive scenario: The investigations quickly clarify the situation and the facility is deemed safe, with possible improvements in procedures.
- Negative scenario: Mistreatment is confirmed and the facility may be temporarily closed, with serious repercussions for the families involved.

### Legal context
The reports were filed based on Swiss laws that protect minors and punish mistreatment. The investigations will follow the legal course provided, with the aim of ascertaining the truth and ensuring justice. According to Article 134 of the Swiss Criminal Code, anyone who mistreats a minor can be punished with a fine or a custodial sentence of up to three years.

### Next steps
The authorities will continue the investigations and provide updates as the situation evolves. Interested families can contact the competent authorities for further information and support.

⚠️ Warning: The information provided in this article is based on official sources and testimonies collected. Families are invited to contact the authorities directly for specific details and updates. ### Analysis of Complaints
The complaints filed against the educational facility 'Arcobaleno' in Chiasso and against the person active in the educational field raise important questions about safety and the quality of educational services in Ticino. The accusations involve alleged physical and psychological abuse, with at least five families having filed formal complaints with the Cantonal Police. It is crucial that the competent authorities conduct thorough investigations to verify the truth of the accusations and ensure that similar episodes do not recur. According to the cantonal legislation of 2019, educational facilities must adhere to strict safety and well-being protocols, with penalties that can go up to 20,000 CHF in case of serious violations.

### Impact on the Community
The news has had a significant impact on the community of Chiasso and surrounding areas, also involving neighboring municipalities such as Novazzano and Balerna. Many families may be concerned about the safety of their children and may request more information and transparency from the authorities. It is important that the Canton and educational institutions work together to restore trust and ensure a safe environment for all children. A concrete example is the request for a public meeting organized by the Municipality of Chiasso for October 15, where families can express their concerns and receive updates on the investigations.

### Comparison with Other Facilities
This case also raises questions about the practices of other educational facilities in Ticino. It is essential that all facilities follow strict safety and well-being protocols for children. The authorities may want to examine the practices of other facilities to ensure they comply with the required standards. For example, the 'Sole e Luna' facility in Lugano has recently implemented a surveillance system with cameras and an anonymous reporting protocol for parents, which could be a model to follow.

### Operational Checklist for Facilities
- **Staff Training**: Mandatory courses on child safety and well-being, with annual updates.
- Reporting Protocol: Anonymous reporting system for parents and employees.
- Surveillance: Installation of cameras in common areas, with respect for privacy.
- Periodic Checks: Quarterly inspections by the competent authorities.
- Transparency: Regular communication with families about any incidents or changes in procedures.

### Support for Families
Families who entrusted their children to the 'Arcobaleno' facility may need psychological and legal support. The authorities and local organizations should offer resources and assistance to help families overcome this difficult situation. For example, the Municipality of Chiasso has already allocated a fund of 50,000 CHF for psychological and legal support, with a team of psychologists and lawyers available for the affected families.

### Next Steps for Authorities
The authorities must continue to monitor the situation and provide regular updates to the community. It is important that the investigations are conducted transparently and that information is shared with families and the public in a timely manner. An example of good practice is the online portal created by the Canton of Ticino, where families can find daily updates on the investigations and any additional safety measures.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: Facility with rigorous safety protocols and continuous staff training.
- Scenario 2: Facility without clear protocols and untrained staff, with consequent risks for children.
- Scenario 3: Facility with safety protocols but without transparency towards families, resulting in a loss of trust.

In conclusion, it is fundamental that the authorities and educational facilities work together to ensure a safe and transparent environment for all children in Ticino.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do if You Suspect Abuse
If you suspect that a child is being abused, it is crucial to act immediately. Here are the steps to follow:

1. Contact the authorities: Report the suspicion to the local police or the public prosecutor's office. In Ticino, the emergency number for the police is 117 and 112 for emergency services. For formal complaints, the public prosecutor's office of the Canton of Ticino can be contacted at 0800 111 111.

2. Document the evidence: Gather any evidence that can support the report, such as photographs, videos, or testimonies. It is important to keep these proofs safe and present them to the competent authorities. For example, in the municipality of Lugano, the municipal social services can provide support in collecting and managing the evidence.

3. Seek support: Contact local organizations or professionals in the field to obtain psychological and legal support. In Ticino, the Ticino Association for the Protection of Childhood (ATPI) offers consulting and support services. Additionally, the Family Counseling Center (CCF) in Bellinzona provides psychological and legal assistance.

4. Protect the child: Ensure that the child is safe and away from the dangerous environment. In emergency cases, you can contact the child protection service of the Canton of Ticino, which can intervene to ensure the child's safety.

### Useful Resources
- Local police: Contact the police in Chiasso for urgent reports. The emergency number is 117.
- Public prosecutor's office: Report to the public prosecutor's office for formal complaints. The phone number is 0800 111 111.
- Support organizations: Seek assistance from local organizations that offer support to families. ATPI and CCF are two examples of organizations that provide psychological and legal support.

### Operational Checklist
- **Contact the authorities**: Emergency number 117 for the police and 112 for emergency services.
- Document the evidence: Collect photographs, videos, and testimonies.
- Seek support: Contact ATPI or CCF for assistance.
- Protect the child: Contact the child protection service of the Canton of Ticino.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
In a case that occurred in Lugano in 2022, a three-year-old child was found with signs of physical abuse. The authorities intervened quickly thanks to a timely report, and the child was placed under the protection of the municipal social services. In another case in Bellinzona, a teacher noticed signs of abuse on a student and immediately contacted the child protection service, which launched an investigation.

### Legislation
In Ticino, the law on the protection of childhood and youth (LPI) of 2008 stipulates that anyone who suspects abuse must report it to the competent authorities. The law provides for fines of up to 20,000 francs for those who do not report a known case of abuse.

### Conclusion
This case underscores the importance of ensuring the safety and well-being of children in educational facilities. The authorities must continue to monitor the situation and provide support to the families concerned. If you need further information or assistance, do not hesitate to contact the competent authorities or use the [support calculator](nav:calculator) to obtain additional resources.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/cronaca/1921866/maltrattamenti-accertamenti-corso-denunce-chiasso)*

item.title: Gender equality that scares Ticino cross-border workers
item.description: Only 28 countries in the world are led by a woman. In Ticino, gender equality is still a distant goal.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Only 28 countries in the world are led by a woman
- Gender equality is still a distant goal in Ticino
- The fight is for parity, not supremacy

## Key facts
- **What**: Gender equality
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Ticino, Switzerland
- Who: Elda Pianezzi, laRegione.ch
- Amount: 28 countries led by women

The data shows a traditionalist world, where decisions are still predominantly made by men. The fight for gender equality is a complex and delicate issue, especially in contexts like the Canton of Ticino, where the presence of cross-border workers is significant. Gender equality does not mean the supremacy of one gender over another, but balance and equal opportunities for all.

### Ticino Context
In Ticino, gender equality is still a distant goal. According to the data, only 28% of managerial positions are held by women.
